tickets.summary: Login button unresponsive on Safari after session timeout
tickets.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Log in and stay idle until the session expires (30 min).
2. The timeout modal appears. Dismiss it.
3. Click the Login button.
Actual: nothing happens. Browser console shows TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'token') in auth-refresh.js.
Expected: user is redirected to the login page and can re-authenticate.

comments.author: Priya
comments.body: Reproduced on Safari 18.2 only, Chrome and Firefox are fine. Looks Safari-specific event handling.

comments.author: Rahul
comments.body: Root cause: the session refresh handler is not re-attached after the timeout modal closes. Fix planned in auth-refresh.js, re-bind on modal dismiss.

tickets.summary: Checkout E2E test flaky: intermittent NoSuchElementException on coupon field
tickets.description:
CheckoutTest.applyCouponTest fails on roughly 30% of Jenkins runs with org.openqa.selenium.NoSuchElementException on selector #coupon-input.
The coupon banner is injected asynchronously 1-2s after page load, so the locator races the banner fetch. Retries are currently masking the failure in nightly runs.

comments.author: Priya
comments.body: Confirmed race condition. Replace the fixed Thread.sleep with an explicit wait on presenceOfElementLocated(#coupon-input), and add @flaky quarantine tag until fixed.

tickets.summary: A/B experiment goal tracking mismatch between dashboard and raw events
tickets.description:
For experiments with revenue goals, the dashboard conversion count is ~4% lower than raw event exports for the same date range. Timezone boundary handling (UTC vs account timezone) suspected.
Resolution: dashboard aggregation was bucketing by UTC day while exports used account timezone. Fixed by normalizing both to account timezone.

comments.author: Dev team
comments.body: Fix shipped in reporting-service 4.12. Regression test added: RTM item R-114 maps to TC-3321 and TC-3322.
